title: Report an incident involving a consumer product or cosmetic: Consumers - Canada.ca
content:
## Report an incident involving a consumer product or cosmetic: Consumers
- Report an incident - consumers
- Report an incident - industry
- Report an incident - fire and safety authorities
- Reporting other issues, contact us
- How to use the consumer report form
Health Canada encourages the public, including health professionals on behalf of their patients or clients, to report a problem with a consumer product or cosmetic if:
- someone has died, been injured or nearly injured, or has a bad reaction from using it
- someone may be harmed because it doesn't work like it's supposed to
- safety instructions or warning labels are missing, confusing or wrong
- property has been damaged
When you submit a report, you will be asked for:
- a description of what happened
- the date and nature of the incident
- the product's brand, name and description
- the type of injury that occurred and any treatment that was needed
- the web address (URL) and/or other identifying numbers, such as the Amazon Standard Identification Number (ASIN) and purchase order number, if the product was purchased online
- any photos you have of the product, product labels and damage caused
You will also be asked:
- where you got the product
- if you wish to remain anonymous or are willing to be contacted if we have any questions
Note: Health Canada publishes recalls and safety alerts . You do not need to submit a report to notify us about a recall that's already published.
If the problem involves an adverse reaction to a drug or medical device, a food safety or labelling issue, pesticides or unfair trade practices visit Reporting other issues .
Submit a consumer report
What happens after you submit your report

## How to use the consumer report form
You should save your form as an .hcxs file on your computer after you have completed and submitted it. Include the case and submission numbers in the file name to help you easily find your form again. These numbers are created once you click the "Submit" button in the form. You may save your incomplete form at any time and upload it later to complete and submit it.
If you saved a copy of the form, you can update it using these instructions:
- Select "Update a report".
- Click "Choose File" to select previously saved .hcxs file.
- Select the file you wish to upload.
- Click "Upload Information".
- A pop-up box will appear confirming that the information was uploaded. Click "Ok".
- Click on "Next" to proceed to the form.
If you only have your case number, you can still update your previous submission. However, you will need to complete all the required fields again.
Update your form using these instructions:
- Locate the case number and enter it into the field labelled "Case Number".
- Click on "Next" to proceed to the form.
- Complete all required fields in the form and "Submit".
To view a read-only version of a form saved in the .hcxs format, follow these instructions.
- Click on the following link: Submit a consumer report.
- A new page called "Consumer incident reporting form" will appear.
- Scroll down the page to section titled "I am here to…". Choose the button titled "View a read-only version of the report".
- In the box that appears below, use the "Choose File/Browse" button to select the .hcxs file you saved on your computer before.
- Click on the "Upload Information" button. A new box will appear. The box will confirm that the consumer's report was uploaded to the online form.
- The box will confirm that the consumer's report was uploaded to the online form.
- In the box, click on the "Okay" button.
- Scroll to the bottom of the page. Click on the "Next" button.
- The first page of the form titled "What happened?" will appear.
- To view the next or previous page of the form, scroll to the bottom of the page. Click on the "Next" or "Previous" button.
If your completed form including attached digital files is larger than 70MB you will see an error message and be unable to submit. If this happens to you, follow these steps:
- Submit attached digital files up to 70MB on your first form.
- Note the case number you receive when you submit that form.
- Go to the " Additional Documents " web form and attach the additional digital files and enter the case number.
